studyGroup.name: CS Sophomores Study Circle
studyGroup.description: A small standing study group for the sophomore CS core. We meet in person at the library when we can and on a shared voice channel when we cannot. Bring questions, not finished answers. We explain things to each other until they stick.

session.notes: Drew the treasure hunt diagram. Everyone implemented insert and delete by hand first.

session.topic: Recursion and the base case leap
session.notes: Factorial three ways, then mapped it to tree traversal. Stop tracing the whole tree.
Two-children case with the in-order successor is where everyone slows down. Drew each step.
Base case, inductive hypothesis, prove the next step. Labeled each part clearly.
Mixed problem set. One person explains, the rest poke holes. Best format we have found.

session.topic: Fall midterm prep, algorithms and operating systems

groundRules.rule: Make the person do the work. Explaining is not the same as solving for them.
No shared code on graded assignments. Concepts only.
Start with the simplest case. One node, two nodes, then build up.
If you are lost, say so early. Nobody here has it all figured out.
